title: salix vulpina
intro: Salix vulpina, also known as the fox willow or coyote willow, is a species of shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix vulpina have been found to provide various health benefits, including relief from pain and fever, as well as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing. Salix vulpina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, sore throats, and skin irritations.
